subject: Re: Is dose weighting performed on tiltseries in WarpTools?
body:
Hi Dimitri and Alister,

Thanks for the responses, very insightful!

Cheers,

Maurice

Op ma 13 okt 2025 om 03:56 schreef teg...@gmail.com <tegunov@gmail.com>:

> Dose weighting is used throughout Warp and M. When weighting tilt movie
> frames to produce the movie average during e.g. fs_motion_and_ctf,
> dose_frame(n) - dose_frame(0) (where dose_frame(0) is the accumulated dose
> from the beginning of the tilt series by the time the first frame of that
> movie is acquired) is applied since B factors are additive and the tilt
> series processing will apply dose_frame(0) to the pre-weighted tilt image
> later.
>
> On Sunday, October 12, 2025 at 4:51:12 PM UTC-7 alist...@gmail.com wrote:
>
>> Hi Maurice,
>>
>> Good question! Your statement is a bit simplistic - doing TM well relies
>> on knowing exactly what was done to the data prior to and during
>> reconstruction. Warp knows exactly what it did and accounts for all of that
>> internally, there are some good notes in the Warp paper.
>> What other TM implementations require as input to perform optimally is
>> basically the wild west...
>>
>> Recommended reading if interested:
>> https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/15065679/
>>
>> Cheers,
>>
>> Alister
>>
>> On Sun, 12 Oct 2025 at 16:07, Maurice Frijns <m.a.fri...@gmail.com>
>> wrote:
>>
>>> Hi Allister,
>>>
>>> Thanks for the quick reply, very helpful!
>>>
>>> I’m still fairly new to tomography processing, so I just wanted to
>>> clarify something. I was under the impression that template matching
>>> generally gives better results when performed on dose-weighted tomograms.
>>>
>>> If dose weighting of the tilt series is only applied in M, does that
>>> mean I’d be running template matching on non–dose-weighted tomograms when
>>> using WarpTools-preprocessed data?
>>>
>>> Thanks again for your help!
>>>
>>> Cheers,
>>>
>>> Maurice
>>>
>>> Op zondag 12 oktober 2025 om 22:58:52 UTC+2 schreef alist...@gmail.com:
>>>
>>>> Hi Maurice,
>>>>
>>>> Dose weighting is applied during movie export based on the per-frame
>>>> dose supplied in your input settings file
>>>>
>>>> https://github.com/warpem/warp/blob/656a2d4ff1ed9ab9ecbd6b5cf9d27db8c134284a/WarpLib/Movie/Movie.ExportMovie.cs#L202-L206
>>>>
>>>> Dose weighting is also applied when reconstructing particle series for
>>>> export to RELION
>>>>
>>>> https://github.com/warpem/warp/blob/656a2d4ff1ed9ab9ecbd6b5cf9d27db8c134284a/WarpLib/TiltSeries/TiltSeries.ReconstructParticleSeries.cs#L114-L140
>>>>
>>>> I remember Dimitry saying that the effects of dose weighting being
>>>> applied twice, first to the movie and then cumulatively at the tilt series
>>>> level, were accounted for - I assume this is true but couldn't find it
>>>> quickly poking around the code, it's probably done in M somewhere :-)
>>>>
>>>> Cheers,
>>>>
>>>> Alister
>>>>
>>>> On Sun, 12 Oct 2025 at 10:35, Maurice Frijns <m.a.fri...@gmail.com>
>>>> wrote:
>>>>
>>>>> Hi all,
>>>>>
>>>>> I couldn't find anywhere in the documentation of WarpTools if dose
>>>>> weighting is performed during motion correction for tilt series, I assume
>>>>> not as the required input is not provided. Is that a correct assumption?
>>>>> and if so, is dose weighting performed during a later step?
>>>>>
>>>>> Cheers,
>>>>>
>>>>> Maurice
>>>>>
>>>>> --
